style_observations.observation: Opening setup is direct and peer-warm — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa) lands cleanly. 'What you do:' + scalar framing (two reads, 30-40 minutes) sets no-nonsense tone.

Practitioner-before-pattern sequencing (five people doing the thing before hearing the name for the thing) echoes Module 2 Karpathy-first approach. Ceiling-before-addition framing (see what ONE agent does before adding three) is a Sutherland reframe — shifts from 'is it possible' to 'how much is already possible.'

Subagent mechanic explained in Boris-flat voice (precise, direct, no flourish). Paragraph structure (What it is → How to launch → What you'll see → What you don't need) is operational and builds confidence. Phrasing 'You don't type a special command. You ask Claude, in plain language' earns the mechanic through contrast.

'Claude Code' appears at line 9 without a primer. Per carve-out rule (Module 3 prework, term well-earned by this point), no flag. OpenClaw has parenthetical primer at same line, earning it at first use.
'Subagent' introduced at line 25, earned at line 27 with structural definition ('helper the main agent dispatches…'). One-breath primer tied to what Claude Code does. Fluency builds from there.

notes: File is shared (Bootstrap + AE101 Module 3 prework); Bootstrap register applied.
Carve-outs honored: Claude Code term not flagged per Module 3 earning-already-complete rule; OpenClaw primer satisfies first-use earn.
No `**Prompt**` blocks to evaluate (check_prompts rules do not fire on this file).
Platform claims (subagent mechanism, phrasing 'Launch a subagent to…') verified working per maintainer notes lines 65-66.
Runtime-fork divs (.rt-code / .rt-cowork) present in body prose but not inside fenced prompt blocks; check_prompts.md rule 9 (markdown shout) does not apply.

Practitioner names and domains (simonwillison.net, karpathy.ai, thorstenball.com, mitchellh.com, ghuntley.com) are readable lists, not broken links; prework asks students to browse recent output, so 'find his piece' is acceptable framing.
